# next-supa-mcp

> MCP Server — Official AI Companion for next-supa-utils

next-supa-mcp is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to ensure AI assistants always generate Next.js + Supabase code using the `next-supa-utils` library instead of raw boilerplate.

## Installation

```bash
npm install -g next-supa-mcp
```

Or clone and build from source:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/aryaintarann/next-supa-mcp.git
cd next-supa-mcp
npm install
npm run build
```

## Configuration

### Claude Desktop

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "next-supa-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "next-supa-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Cursor

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "next-supa-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "next-supa-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### VS Code (Copilot)

```json
{
  "servers": {
    "next-supa-mcp": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "next-supa-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Windsurf

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "next-supa-mcp": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "next-supa-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Tools

### get_next_supa_utils_docs

Returns the official cheatsheet and usage guide for the next-supa-utils library. Use this FIRST before writing any Next.js + Supabase code.

No input required.

**Output includes:**

Client-side hooks:

```typescript
import {
  SupaProvider,      // Optional context provider for custom Supabase URL/Key
  useSupaUser,       // Get the current authenticated user
  useSupaSession,    // Get the current session object
  useSupaUpload,     // Upload files to Supabase Storage
  useSupaRealtime,   // Subscribe to real-time Postgres changes
} from 'next-supa-utils/client';
```

Server-side helpers:

```typescript
import {
  withSupaAuth,    // Auth middleware for Next.js middleware.ts
  createAction,    // Wrapper for Server Actions with auto-injected supabase client
  routeWrapper,    // Wrapper for Route Handlers (GET, POST, etc.)
} from 'next-supa-utils/server';
```

### scaffold_auth_middleware

Generates a complete Next.js `middleware.ts` file that uses `withSupaAuth` from `next-supa-utils/server`.

**Input Schema:**

```typescript
{
  routes: Array<{
    path: string;              // e.g. "/dashboard" or "/admin/:path*"
    allowedRoles?: string[];   // e.g. ["admin", "editor"]
  }>;
  redirectTo?: string;         // Default: "/login"
}
```

**Example Input:**

```json
{
  "routes": [
    { "path": "/dashboard" },
    { "path": "/admin/:path*", "allowedRoles": ["admin"] }
  ],
  "redirectTo": "/login"
}
```

**Example Output:**

```typescript
// middleware.ts
import { withSupaAuth } from 'next-supa-utils/server';

export default withSupaAuth({
  routes: [
    { path: '/dashboard' },
    { path: '/admin/:path*', allowedRoles: ['admin'] },
  ],
  redirectTo: '/login',
});

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)'],
};
```

### generate_crud_action

Generates a Next.js Server Action function using `createAction` from `next-supa-utils/server`. The `createAction` wrapper automatically injects the Supabase client as the first argument and returns `{ data, error }`.

**Input Schema:**

```typescript
{
  tableName: string;                          // e.g. "posts", "users"
  operation: "insert" | "update" | "delete";  // CRUD operation
}
```

**Example Input:**

```json
{
  "tableName": "posts",
  "operation": "insert"
}
```

**Example Output (insert):**

```typescript
'use server';

import { createAction } from 'next-supa-utils/server';

interface CreatePostsInput {
  title: string;
  content?: string;
}

export const createPosts = createAction(
  async (supabase, input: CreatePostsInput) => {
    const { data, error } = await supabase
      .from('posts')
      .insert(input)
      .select()
      .single();

    if (error) throw error;
    return data;
  }
);
```

**Example Output (update):**

```typescript
'use server';

import { createAction } from 'next-supa-utils/server';

interface UpdatePostsInput {
  id: string;
  title?: string;
  content?: string;
}

export const updatePosts = createAction(
  async (supabase, input: UpdatePostsInput) => {
    const { id, ...updateData } = input;

    const { data, error } = await supabase
      .from('posts')
      .update(updateData)
      .eq('id', id)
      .select()
      .single();

    if (error) throw error;
    return data;
  }
);
```

**Example Output (delete):**

```typescript
'use server';

import { createAction } from 'next-supa-utils/server';

export const deletePosts = createAction(
  async (supabase, id: string) => {
    const { error } = await supabase
      .from('posts')
      .delete()
      .eq('id', id);

    if (error) throw error;
    return { success: true };
  }
);
```

**Client-side usage of createAction results:**

```typescript
// All createAction functions return { data, error }
const result = await createPosts({ title: 'Hello World' });

if (result.error) {
  // result.error is a SupaError with message, code, etc.
  console.error(result.error.message);
  return;
}

// result.data is correctly typed
console.log(result.data);
```

### read_supabase_schema

Reads the user's Supabase TypeScript schema file to understand real table and column names. Use this BEFORE generating CRUD actions.

**Input Schema:**

```typescript
{
  filePath?: string;  // Default: "types/database.types.ts"
}
```

**Example Input:**

```json
{
  "filePath": "types/database.types.ts"
}
```

Returns the contents of the schema file so the AI can generate accurate code.

## next-supa-utils API Reference

### withSupaAuth (Server Middleware)

```typescript
import { withSupaAuth } from 'next-supa-utils/server';

// Basic usage - protect routes
export default withSupaAuth({
  routes: [
    { path: '/dashboard' },
    { path: '/admin/:path*', allowedRoles: ['admin'] },
  ],
  redirectTo: '/login',
});
```

Full options:

```typescript
withSupaAuth({
  routes: RouteConfig[];          // Protected routes with optional RBAC
  redirectTo?: string;            // Default: "/login"
  publicRoutes?: string[];        // Routes that bypass auth
  onAuthSuccess?: Function;       // Callback after successful auth
  roleExtractor?: Function;       // Custom role extraction logic
  supabaseUrl?: string;           // Custom Supabase URL
  supabaseAnonKey?: string;       // Custom anon key
});

interface RouteConfig {
  path: string;
  allowedRoles?: string[];
}
```

### createAction (Server Actions)

```typescript
import { createAction } from 'next-supa-utils/server';

// createAction auto-injects supabase as first argument
export const getProfile = createAction(
  async (supabase, userId: string) => {
    const { data, error } = await supabase
      .from('profiles')
      .select()
      .eq('id', userId)
      .single();

    if (error) throw error;
    return data;
  }
);
```

Signature:

```typescript
function createAction<TArgs extends unknown[], TResult>(
  fn: (supabase: SupabaseClient, ...args: TArgs) => Promise<TResult>
): (...args: TArgs) => Promise<ActionResponse<TResult>>

type ActionResponse<T> =
  | { data: T; error: null }
  | { data: null; error: SupaError };
```

### useSupaUser (Client Hook)

```typescript
'use client';
import { useSupaUser } from 'next-supa-utils/client';

export default function Dashboard() {
  const { user, isLoading } = useSupaUser();

  if (isLoading) return <p>Loading...</p>;
  if (!user) return <p>Not authenticated</p>;

  return <p>Welcome, {user.email}</p>;
}
```

### useSupaSession (Client Hook)

```typescript
'use client';
import { useSupaSession } from 'next-supa-utils/client';

export default function SessionInfo() {
  const { session, isLoading } = useSupaSession();

  if (isLoading) return <p>Loading...</p>;

  return <p>Token expires at: {session?.expires_at}</p>;
}
```

### SupaProvider (Client Provider - Optional)

```typescript
'use client';
import { SupaProvider } from 'next-supa-utils/client';

// Only needed for custom/multi-tenant Supabase instances
export default function Layout({ children }) {
  return (
    <SupaProvider
      supabaseUrl="https://your-instance.supabase.co"
      supabaseAnonKey="your-anon-key"
    >
      {children}
    </SupaProvider>
  );
}
```

### routeWrapper (Route Handlers)

```typescript
import { routeWrapper } from 'next-supa-utils/server';

export const GET = routeWrapper(async (supabase, request, context) => {
  const { data, error } = await supabase
    .from('posts')
    .select();

  if (error) throw error;
  return data;
});
```

## AI Rules

When using next-supa-utils, AI assistants MUST follow these rules:

1. NEVER use `createServerComponentClient` or `createRouteHandlerClient` from `@supabase/auth-helpers-nextjs`.
2. NEVER use `@supabase/ssr` directly.
3. ALWAYS use `createAction()` for Server Actions — it auto-injects the supabase client.
4. ALWAYS use `withSupaAuth()` for middleware — export it as default.
5. ALWAYS use `routeWrapper()` for API Route Handlers.
6. ALWAYS use `useSupaUser()` and `useSupaSession()` for client-side auth state.
7. Import client hooks from `next-supa-utils/client`.
8. Import server helpers from `next-supa-utils/server`.
